rationale: The cited paper is used in the core model-development argument, not just as background. In the section specifying Archetype 2, the authors explicitly identify Gilbride et al. (2006) as particularly important evidence that consumers attend to attribute subsets and that information usage varies with attribute presentation. They then position their IAM contribution as extending that line of work by modeling endogenous information-state selection and additional context effects.
relation_description: The citing paper builds directly on Gilbride et al. (2006) and extends it from subset/variable-selection insights to an explicit endogenous activated-information-state choice process with benefit-cost structure and broader contextual determinants.
